stories.title: Filter executed orders by settlement date
stories.description: As a back-office operator, I want to filter executed orders by settlement date, so that I can reconcile end-of-day positions. Acceptance criteria: Given a settlement date, When I apply the filter, Then only orders settling on that date are shown.

stories.title: Export daily trade blotter
stories.description: As a compliance analyst, I want to export the daily trade blotter to CSV, so that I can submit the regulatory report. Acceptance criteria: Given the blotter, When I click export, Then a CSV of executed trades is produced.

stories.title: Immutable amendment log
stories.description: As an auditor, I want an immutable log of order amendments, so that I can trace who changed what. Acceptance criteria: Given an amendment, When it is saved, Then an entry with user, timestamp and old/new values is recorded.

stories.title: CSV export of the blotter
stories.description: I want a CSV export of the blotter so that auditors can reconcile trades.

stories.title: Netting refactor with dependency
stories.description: As a developer, I want the netting refactor, so that fees net correctly. Acceptance criteria: Given a trade, When fees apply, Then they net per account. It depends on the pricing service upgrade being delivered first.

stories.title: Real-time position view
stories.description: As a desk trader, I want real-time positions per instrument, so that I can manage intraday risk. Acceptance criteria: Given an open position, When the price updates, Then the unrealised P&L refreshes within two seconds.

stories.title: Limit-breach alert
stories.description: As a risk manager, I want a limit-breach alert, so that I can react quickly. The alert should be reliable.

stories.title: Trading dashboard
stories.description: We need a fast and user-friendly dashboard that shows everything about trades and also exports data and also sends email alerts and integrates with the risk system.

stories.title: Settlement failure flagging
stories.description: As a settlement clerk, I want to flag failed settlements, so that I can chase the counterparty. Acceptance criteria: Given a settlement marked failed, When I open the failures view, Then the item appears with its counterparty and amount.
